rulings.Case Title: VERA V WITIUK VS. JANE KNOP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2019 line 3. PLAINTIFF VERA WITIUK's Motion To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to properly and adequately address (even after being given an opportunity to provide supplemental briefing) its entitlement to be substituted in. A successor in interest is the proper substitute plaintiff only if there is no personal representative who has a superior right to be substituted for the decedent. CCP 377.31, CCP 377.32. No authority is provided and no argument is made as to Personal Representative's in Canadian probate action rights and obligations in regards to this action. The Court also notes that no notice of this motion was provided to the Personal Representative. As this motion is denied without prejudice, moving party may file another motion if and when appropriate in light of this ruling. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. COURTHOUSE VENTURES, INC. ET AL

rulings.Case Title: MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. COURTHOUSE VENTURES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Appoint Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF MOAB INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC's Motion To Appoint Receiver The Court is inclined to deny the motion for appointment of receiver at this time. However, since parties agree that the property should be sold, the parties shall agree on a broker to proceed with the sale. If parties are unable to agree on a broker by June 14, 2019, parties shall submit the names of 2 brokers each to the Court by no later than June 21, 2019. The sale proceeds from the sale of California property shall be deposited with the Court; the sale proceeds attributable to MoAB from the sale of Germania property, except for the shares of Antrea, et al., to be deposited with the Court. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANA CLEGG VS. WILMINGTON TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT FAY SERVICING, LLC and WILMINGTON TRUST's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is denied as to the request for punitive damages. Sufficient facts are alleged to support a request for punitive damages based on fraud. Motion is GRANTED as to the request and references to emotional distress damages. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for a proposition that at trust, as opposed to an individual, may recover emotional distress damages. Green v. Central Mortgage (2015) 148 F. Supp.3d 852 is not on point. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. ERNEST MIDDLEBROOK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 21, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT ERNEST MIDDLEBROOK's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Court lacks jurisdiction in the absence of proper verification. Further, verification is sufficient per CCP 446.= (501/CFH)
